item.title: Iran Unwilling to Talk About Opening Hormuz While Under Attack
item.description: Iranian officials have become reluctant to even discuss reopening the Strait of Hormuz as they focus on surviving the US-Israeli onslaught, according to a person involved in direct, high-level contacts with Tehran.

item.title: One Veteran Director Has Some Thoughts on How to Build Video Games Faster
item.description: Veteran game director Josh Sawyer thinks developers need to make fewer big changes during production

item.title: Dallas Fed Sees Hit to Global GDP if Strait of Hormuz Stays Shut
item.description: A halt in Strait of Hormuz oil shipments that persists through June due to the Iran war would reduce global economic growth by an annualized 2.9 percentage points in the second quarter, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of Dallas.

item.title: Trump Approves NextEra Gas Expansion to Meet Rising Power Demand
item.description: NextEra Energy Inc. plans to build up to 10 gigawatts of natural gas-fired generation in Texas and Pennsylvania to meet soaring demand driven in part by the data center boom.

item.title: Saudi Crude Oil Shipments From Yanbu Ease Following Record Surge
item.description: Saudi Arabia’s crude loadings from the port of Yanbu on the Red Sea have shown a modest pullback over the past two days, following a record ramp-up as Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: China Has Already Notched its First Major Win From the Iran War
item.description: BYD sales are already surging

item.title: Reeves to Lose £3 Billion of Fiscal Buffer Amid Gilts Selloff
item.description: The surge in UK gilt yields on the back of the Iran war could cost Chancellor of the Exchequer Rachel Reeves more than a 10th of her fiscal buffer by increasing the cost of servicing government debt, according to Bloomberg economists.

item.title: Bond Market’s Big 2026 Fed Bet Flipped on Its Head by Oil Surge
item.description: Bond traders are scrambling for a new strategy after the oil-driven inflation shock triggered by the war in Iran scuppered the popular bet on further interest-rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

item.title: Echelon Data Centres Owners Said to Weigh Options Including Sale
item.description: The owners of Echelon Data Centres are considering options including selling part or all of their stakes in the Dublin-based company, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Soy Poised for Worst Week Since 2024 as US-China Meeting Delayed
item.description: Soybean futures were headed for the biggest weekly decline since July 2024 as American exports once again stall after US President Donald Trump postponed his meeting with Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

item.title: Lincoln National Said to Seek Deal to Cut Life Insurance Risk
item.description: Lincoln National Corp. is seeking a reinsurance deal that would shift billions of dollars of life insurance reserves off its balance sheet, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran War Hits Chinese, Russian Fertilizer Exports to Nigeria
item.description: China and Russia are delaying fertilizer shipments to Nigeria as the Iran war shakes up the global supply chain for the crucial crop nutrients.

item.title: Mexico Finance Chief Downplays Fiscal Effects of Oil Turmoil
item.description: Mexico’s Finance Minister Edgar Amador said the fiscal effects from rising oil prices due to the war in Iran will be neutral.

item.title: UK Energy Price Cap Seen Jumping 20% as Iran War Roils Market
item.description: The UK’s cap on energy prices is expected to surge 20% this summer, heaping more pressure on Prime Minister Keir Starmer to soften the blow before it lands.

item.title: Billionaire Batista in Talks to Rescue Brazil Steel Tycoon
item.description: Brazilian magnate Joesley Batista is making a play for the cement unit of Benjamin Steinbruch’s troubled conglomerate CSN, according to people familiar with the talks.

item.title: SoftBank Plans Giant Ohio AI Data Center Powered by Gas Plants
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. is working to build a massive AI data center on federally owned land in Ohio that it’s planning to power with roughly $33 billion worth of natural gas-fired electricity to be installed by the end of the decade.

item.title: Treasury’s Record Interventions Challenge Brazil Financing Plan
item.description: The Brazilian Treasury’s record interventions in local markets are eating into a liquidity cushion seen as key to managing public debt risks.

item.title: How the Iran War Is Disrupting Global Oil and Gas Supply
item.description: Oil and gas markets have been rocked by disruption to supply out of the Persian Gulf. Prices of both commodities have jumped since the US and Israel began their strikes on Iran.

item.title: Why Diesel Prices Are the Real Concern for the Economy
item.description: No one can stop talking about sky-high gasoline prices. The ripple effects of diesel are worth watching too.

item.title: Oil Tanker Market’s Key Rate Thrown Into Chaos With Hormuz Shut
item.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has thrown a vital part of the shipping market into chaos.

item.title: Nexstar Plans $5.1 Billion Bond Sale for Tegna Deal, Lowers Loan
item.description: Nexstar Media Group Inc. plans to sell $5.12 billion of bonds to help fund the acquisition of fellow TV-station owner Tegna Inc., marking a shift in the financing plans for the deal.

item.title: ECB Asks Banks How Iran War Is Impacting Clients, Operations
item.description: The European Central Bank is asking lenders about fallout from the Iran war on their operations and clients, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Iran War’s Energy Shock Puts Some Major IMF Borrowers at Risk
item.description: Conflict in the Middle East is sending shockwaves across the developing world, raising the prospect that emerging economies will turn to the International Monetary Fund for more help.

item.title: xAI Sends Engineers to Client Sites to Win Business from OpenAI
item.description: Elon Musk’s xAI is sending engineers directly into the offices of prospective corporate clients in an attempt to claw business customers away from rivals OpenAI and Anthropic PBC.

item.title: Bowman Still Projecting Three Interest-Rate Cuts Before Year-End
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s top bank cop still supports three interest-rate cuts in 2026 and said she expects strong economic growth this year but is keeping an eye on the impact on the war in Iran.

item.title: White House Unveils AI Legislative Plan for Skeptical Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump released a national framework for regulating artificial intelligence on Friday, laying the groundwork for Congress to create a federal standard for the rapidly growing technology.

item.title: Fed’s Waller Cautious On Oil, May Advocate Rate Cuts Later
item.description: Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller said he is cautious about how surging oil prices on the back of the Iran war will impact inflation, though a weak job market may still call for interest-rate cuts later this year.

item.title: Meta, Alphabet Join Credit-Risk Index as AI Hedging Demand Soars
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc., Alphabet Inc. and Microsoft Corp. joining an index of high-grade firms’ credit default swaps is another sign of investors increasingly hedging hyperscalers’ debt amid surging bond sales.

item.title: Irish Economy in Better Position Than European Peers on Iran War Risk, Makhlouf Says
item.description: Ireland is better placed than many of its European peers to weather economic risks from the Iran war fallout as it starts from a stronger base, Central Bank of Ireland Governor Gabriel Makhlouf said.

item.title: Ecolab to Buy Data Center Cooling Firm CoolIT From KKR Funds
item.description: Ecolab Inc. has agreed to buy CoolIT Systems Inc., a company that develops cooling technology for AI data centers, in a $4.75 billion all-cash deal.
